name: [Your Name]
title: Power Electronics PhD Researcher
tagline: Designing efficient converters, robust digital control, and lab-ready validation workflows.

hero.headline: I build power-electronics systems that connect converter hardware, digital control, and reliable validation.
hero.subheadline: PhD researcher in power electronics focused on high-efficiency conversion, practical control implementation, and turning research questions into repeatable lab prototypes.

quickFacts.label: Now
quickFacts.detail: Building modular hardware benches for repeatable experiments.

quickFacts.label: Method
quickFacts.detail: Connecting simulation, embedded control, and verification.

quickFacts.label: Why it matters
quickFacts.detail: Making advanced power-electronics research easier to validate and deploy.

currentFocus.theme: Converter architectures and control workflows that stay robust from simulation to hardware validation.
currentFocus.importance: Power-electronics research becomes more useful when control ideas can move into repeatable, instrumented, and well-documented prototypes instead of staying only in simulation.
currentFocus.now: My current work centers on modular converter platforms, practical digital control implementation, and evaluation workflows that make debugging and comparison faster.

projects.title: Modular bidirectional converter platform
projects.summary: A flexible bench for testing topologies, sensors, and control strategies with consistent measurement interfaces.

projects.title: Digital control validation workflow
projects.summary: A repeatable path from simulation models to embedded code, with hardware-oriented checkpoints for tuning and debugging.

projects.title: Wide-bandgap device evaluation rig
projects.summary: Comparing switching behavior, thermal considerations, and gate-drive decisions under realistic lab conditions.

methods: Hardware bring-up checklists and lab documentation
goals: Shorten the gap between simulation and hardware results
Create reusable converter and measurement infrastructure
Document procedures so future experiments scale better

skillGroups.title: Power Stage Design
skillGroups.description: Designing the hardware side of converter systems with attention to efficiency, measurement access, and safe bring-up.

skillGroups.title: Control and Modeling
skillGroups.description: Moving from equations to stable control implementation with a clear validation path.

skillGroups.title: Software and Lab Workflow
skillGroups.description: Building repeatable engineering workflows across software, firmware, and documentation.
items: Technical writing for manuals and bring-up guides

cv.profile: This site is structured as a research portfolio first: it shows what I am working on now, how my experience developed over time, and how I turn ideas into documented engineering work.

education.focus: Power electronics, converter control, and laboratory validation
Advanced power conversion and system modeling
Electrical engineering foundations and hardware practice

strengths: Turning abstract research problems into testable engineering questions
Building converter prototypes with clear measurement strategies
Documenting workflows so projects stay reusable over time
collaboration: Open to academic collaboration, technical discussion, and project exchange
Comfortable contributing to prototype design, validation planning, and technical documentation
This page is ready to be paired with a downloadable PDF CV later

contact.title: Interested in collaboration or technical exchange?
contact.text: I am happy to connect around converter design, digital control, experimental workflows, and documentation-heavy research projects.
